item.title: HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this? — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this?

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-04-12 19:51 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

Early replies (first 3)

62107190
It's funny because I'm supposed to be an Europoor but my life standars is better than 99% of Americans lmfao.

I've never heard a single European complaining about debt. But you meet one of those Amerimutts that come to our continent for tourism or to teach English, and 5 minutes into the conversation they will start telling how how much in debt they are. BAFFLING.

62107202
>>62107169
>HCOL city
>$820 rent utilities included
and then your dad stopped molesting you and the out of body disassociation cope ended.

62107317
>>62107169
>>62107190
I'm American. Envious, ungrateful, whiny, bitchy Americans are an embarrassment. It's really not hard to live here at all. The problem is almost every retard that lives here thinks you should be able to afford a 2,300 sqft house on 1 acre of land, while having a new Toyota Camry in the driveway and enough in savings to go on 2 - 3 week-long vacations a year after putting 15% into an investment account, all on a McDonald's wage, or a basic-bitch corporate cubicle job, while only working 30 hours a week (yes, even 40 hours is too much work for Americans now).

Most people live WAY above their means. It's just a mixture of low-IQ combined with a set of personality traits that make them susceptible to short-term thinking and hedonism. Then they beg for free-gibs from the government while producing less material goods and services. It doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out where this leads to.

I feel zero pity or sympathy for any of these people. Fuck them and their retarded choices.

item.title: been following the hormuz situation and kept seeing claims about iran collecting btc from oil tankers.. — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
been following the hormuz situation and kept seeing claims about iran collecting btc from oil tankers..

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-12 19:44 UTC

OP

been following the hormuz situation and kept seeing claims about iran collecting btc from oil tankers.. couldn't find any solid numbers so i just built something to track it

https://straitwatch.lovable.app

what it shows right now:

182 vessels tracked in the region

estimated ~₿475/day collected via the toll — based on AIS vessel data and bloomberg/ft reporting, not actual blockchain data

5 OFAC sanctioned ships active in the corridor

war premium model sitting at $9.88/barrel = roughly $170M/day extra cost globally

in 12 days since april 1 the model estimates ₿4,530 accumulated.. that's equivalent to 10 days of global bitcoin mining output without running a single miner

what's actually interesting to me isn't the btc amount.. it's that this works completely outside swift, ofac, all of it. by the time any bank knows a transaction happened it's already settled

not claiming this is verified blockchain data — full methodology is in the dashboard. built it for transparency

Early replies (first 3)

62107872
Long story short: Bitcoin will hit $1 Million

62107884
accepting payment in bitcoin and immediately dumping it for something else isn't gonna impact the price much
it's not like iran wants to hoard btc lol

62108024
If Iran gets any significant amount of Bitcoin the price will hust be dumped immediately.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62108039
>US navy controls strait
>no tolls to Iran
well you tried op. not that your thesis ever made sense anyway.

>and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers
Actually, even this is not completely really true to be honest. The whole point of DOT 1.0 was to be able to make L2s. That was done and complete and there's like 40 L2s running on DOT. Then DOT 2.0 came and with it came Asset hub. Asset hub is a COMMON GOOD L2 that can run, as you've mentioned, EVM smart contracts and also PVM smart contracts which are SUPERIOR to EVM because the use the RISC-V architecture. You literally make it seem like launching EVM compatibility is a bad choice where in reality it's a move forward in order to make the chain more flexible.

https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864 Deploy Universal Deterministic Deployment Proxy (CREATE2 Factory) on Asset Hub
This ref passed today making it so that people can launch their EVM smart contracts on the same address as they have on ETH or other L2s. So Uniswap will soon launch on Asset Hub on the same address as they're on ETH or any other L2.

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

That would mean that the Community staking pool would receive $27.375B a year to distribute. That's not farfetched if we expect the Chainlink Network to collectively process 1 Quadrillion dollars in transactions (i.e. more than the DTCC) a year. That's 3.65*10^-3 % of the transactions. Suppose Chainlink takes 0.01% of the Quadrillion dollars as revenue for the network, 10^-4 *10^12 = 10^8. yeah we're short to be able to pay out to the staking pool which would require $27.375*10^9. Might have to cut that down to 10 cents a link a day.
